# System Design {#sec-system-design}

This chapter presents the comprehensive system design of UpHeal, covering requirements analysis, architectural decisions, detailed diagrams, and security considerations.

## Functional Requirements {#sec-functional-requirements}

The following functional requirements define the core capabilities of the UpHeal system:

-   **FR-01**: The system shall allow users to register and authenticate securely using email and password.
-   **FR-02**: The system shall provide an AI-powered conversational interface that responds to the user's mental health concerns with clinically grounded, empathetic responses.
-   **FR-03**: The system shall detect the user's emotional state from text input and adapt its response style accordingly.
-   **FR-04**: The system shall support speech-to-text input via the Whisper model for accessibility and convenience.
-   **FR-05**: The system shall allow users to log their mood on a daily basis with a 5-level scale and optional journaling.
-   **FR-06**: The system shall provide gamification elements including XP, levels, streaks, badges, achievements, and challenges.
-   **FR-07**: The system shall integrate a retrieval-augmented generation pipeline that grounds LLM responses in curated clinical knowledge (DSM-5-TR, CBT workbooks).
-   **FR-08**: The system shall administer clinical assessments (GAD-7 and PHQ-9) and generate personalized wellness roadmaps.
-   **FR-09**: The system shall store conversation history securely for user review and continuity.
-   **FR-10**: The system shall provide emergency resources with crisis hotline information when crisis keywords are detected.

## Non-Functional Requirements {#sec-non-functional-requirements}

-   **NFR-01**: Response latency for chat interactions shall not exceed 5 seconds under normal conditions.
-   **NFR-02**: The system shall comply with data protection regulations (GDPR principles and Egyptian Data Protection Law).
-   **NFR-03**: The application shall be cross-platform (Android and iOS) via a single Flutter codebase.
-   **NFR-04**: The system shall maintain 99.5% uptime for backend services.
-   **NFR-05**: All sensitive data shall be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3).
-   **NFR-06**: The system shall support 1,000 concurrent users with horizontal scaling.
-   **NFR-07**: The clinical auditor shall process every response within 500ms.

## Use Case Diagram {#sec-use-case}

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    User[User] --> UC1[Register/Login]
    User --> UC2[Start Chat Session]
    User --> UC3[Log Mood]
    User --> UC4[View Progress & Badges]
    User --> UC5[Take Clinical Assessment]
    User --> UC6[View Wellness Roadmap]
    User --> UC7[Use Voice Input]
    User --> UC8[Access Emergency Resources]
    
    System[System] --> UC9[Classify Emotion]
    System --> UC10[Retrieve Clinical Context]
    System --> UC11[Generate RAG-Augmented Response]
    System --> UC12[Audit Response for Safety]
    System --> UC13[Update Gamification State]
    System --> UC14[Store Conversation History]
```

## Sequence Diagram — Chat Interaction {#sec-sequence-diagram}

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Flutter App
    participant B as FastAPI Backend
    participant S as Supabase
    participant C as ChromaDB
    participant L as LLM Service
    participant A as Clinical Auditor

    U->>F: Send message (text or voice)
    F->>B: POST /api/chat {user_id, message, session_id}
    B->>S: Verify JWT token
    S-->>B: Token valid
    B->>B: Emotion classification
    B->>C: Retrieve relevant context (RAG)
    C-->>B: Top-k passages
    B->>L: LLM generation with context + emotion
    L-->>B: Generated response
    B->>A: Audit response for safety
    A-->>B: Safety status (GREEN/YELLOW/RED)
    B->>S: Store conversation turn
    B-->>F: Response + emotion + safety status
    F-->>U: Display response
```

## Functional Flow Diagram {#sec-functional-flow}

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[User Opens App] --> B{Authenticated?}
    B -->|No| C[Login / Register]
    B -->|Yes| D[Home Dashboard]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Select Action]
    E --> F[Chat Session]
    E --> G[Mood Logger]
    E --> H[Clinical Assessment]
    E --> I[Progress & Badges]
    E --> J[Emergency Resources]
    F --> K[Send Message]
    K --> L[Emotion Classification]
    L --> M[RAG Context Retrieval]
    M --> N[LLM Response Generation]
    N --> O[Clinical Audit]
    O --> P{Safe?}
    P -->|Yes| Q[Display Response]
    P -->|No| R[Show Emergency Resources]
    Q --> S[Update Gamification]
    S --> F
    G --> T[Save Mood Entry]
    T --> S
    H --> U[Generate Roadmap]
    U --> S
```

## System Architecture {#sec-architecture}

The UpHeal system follows a layered microservices architecture:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    subgraph Frontend
        A[Flutter Mobile App]
    end
    subgraph Gateway
        B[FastAPI Gateway]
        B1[Auth Middleware]
        B2[Orchestrator]
    end
    subgraph Microservices
        C[Assessment Service]
        D[Chat Service]
        E[Journal Service]
        F[Telemetry Service]
        G[Roadmap Service]
    end
    subgraph AI Pipeline
        H[ChromaDB Vector Store]
        I[Sentence-Transformers]
        J[LLM Service]
        K[Clinical Auditor]
    end
    subgraph Data
        L[Supabase PostgreSQL]
        M[Supabase Storage]
        N[Supabase Auth]
    end

    A --> B
    B --> B1
    B --> B2
    B2 --> C
    B2 --> D
    B2 --> E
    B2 --> F
    B2 --> G
    D --> H
    D --> I
    D --> J
    D --> K
    C --> L
    D --> L
    E --> L
    F --> L
    G --> L
    A --> N
```

## AI/ML Pipeline Design {#sec-ai-pipeline}

The AI pipeline processes user input through four stages:

### Stage 1: Input Processing
Text is received directly from the Flutter app; voice input is transcribed using the Whisper speech-to-text model. The transcribed or typed text is passed to the emotion classifier.

### Stage 2: Emotion Classification
The emotion classifier is a fine-tuned model (planned: BERT-based or DistilBERT) trained on a mental health dialogue dataset. It recognizes the following categories: *anxious, sad, angry, neutral, hopeful,* and *distressed*. The model processes each user message and returns a probability distribution over emotion labels. The top label is used to adapt the response strategy.

### Stage 3: RAG Retrieval
The user's message and detected emotion are embedded using `all-mpnet-base-v2` (768 dimensions). The system queries the ChromaDB vector store, which indexes:
- DSM-5-TR (Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, 5th Edition, Text Revision)
- "Mind Over Mood" (CBT workbook)
- "Cognitive Behavior Therapy: Basics and Beyond" (2nd Edition)
- "Managing Obsessions" (clinical guide)
- "Change the Way You Feel" (therapeutic workbook)

The retrieval uses HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) with cosine similarity, `ef_search=128`, `ef_construction=200`, and `M=16`. The system retrieves the top-k relevant passages (k=5-10) filtered by metadata (symptom tags, difficulty level, safety risk).

### Stage 4: Response Generation
The retrieved passages, classified emotion, and conversation history are assembled into a structured prompt for the LLM. The prompt includes:
- System instructions (CBT-based therapist persona)
- Retrieved clinical context
- Detected emotion label
- Conversation history (last 5 turns)
- Current user message

The LLM (currently Groq Cloud's `llama-3.1-8b-instant`, with planned migration to self-hosted Gemma 2B via Ollama on RunPod) generates a response. The response is then passed to the clinical auditor.

### Stage 5: Clinical Audit
The clinical auditor validates the response across five dimensions:
1. **Crisis detection**: Scans for crisis keywords (EN/AR) and assigns GREEN/YELLOW/RED status.
2. **Robotic tone detection**: Ensures the response is empathetic, not clinical or robotic.
3. **Safety risk flagging**: Flags tasks with high safety risk.
4. **Frustration scoring**: Detects user frustration via keyword-based sentiment analysis.
5. **Emergency payload**: If RED status, returns locale-specific hotline numbers and immediate action guidance.

## Data Models and Database Schema {#sec-data-models}

### Supabase PostgreSQL Schema

The system uses Supabase as the primary database, with the following tables:

| Table | Purpose | Key Columns |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `users` | Authentication baseline | id, email, created_at |
| `user_profiles` | Clinical profiles | user_id, screen_time_minutes, gad7_score, phq9_score, user_level, modality_weights (JSONB), tag_boosts (JSONB) |
| `clinical_tasks` | Task definitions | id, title, description, difficulty, xp_reward, modality, locale, clinical_tags, safety_risk |
| `roadmaps` | User roadmaps | user_id, generation_number, overall_theme, status (ACTIVE/COMPLETED/EXPIRED) |
| `roadmap_tasks` | Task-roadmap junction | roadmap_id, task_id, sequence_order, xp_earned, status |
| `assessment_responses` | Assessment history | user_id, form_payload, gad7_score, phq9_score, screen_time_minutes |
| `journal_entries` | Journal entries | id, user_id, title, content, mood, mood_rating, tags, is_archived |
| `chat_sessions` | Chat sessions | id, user_id, roadmap_id |
| `chat_messages` | Chat messages | id, session_id, role, content, metadata |
| `interaction_logs` | Task interaction telemetry | log_id, user_id, task_id, interaction_type (VIEWED/STARTED/COMPLETED/SKIPPED), completion_time |
| `roadmap_mutations` | Director override audit | mutation_id, user_id, directive_id, kind, pre/post_mutation_state (JSONB) |
| `mood_entries` | Mood tracking | id, user_id, mood (1-5), date, timestamp |
| `streak_data` | Streak persistence | user_id, current_streak, longest_streak, freeze_tokens, last_activity_date |

### ChromaDB Vector Store

| Collection | Documents | Embedding Model | Dimensions |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| `clinical_rag_mini` | 4 books (basic) | `all-mpnet-base-v2` | 768 |
| `clinical_rag_mini_enriched` | 4 books (enriched) | `all-mpnet-base-v2` | 768 |

**Metadata per document:** source_file, char_count, page_numbers, header, clinical_tags, tag_primary, difficulty (1-5), xp_reward, safety_risk.

## Gamification System Design {#sec-gamification-design}

### XP and Leveling System

| Activity | Base XP |
|----------|---------|
| Mood log | 5 |
| Breathing exercise | 5 |
| Journal entry | 10 |
| Challenge completion | 10 |
| Focus session | 15 |
| Sleep log | 5 |
| Step goal | 10 |
| AI chat | 5 |

**Level formula:** Level 1→2 requires 100 XP. Each subsequent level adds 50 XP to the threshold.

**Streak multipliers:**

| Streak Days | Multiplier |
|-------------|------------|
| 0–6 | 1.0× |
| 7–13 | 1.25× |
| 14–29 | 1.5× |
| 30–89 | 2.0× |
| 90–179 | 2.5× |
| 180–364 | 3.0× |
| 365+ | 5.0× |

### Badge System

Ten badges in three categories:

| Category | Badge | Requirement |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Streak | Warm-up | 3-day streak |
| Streak | Week Warrior | 7-day streak |
| Streak | Habit Builder | 14-day streak |
| Streak | Month Master | 30-day streak |
| Tasks | Getting Started | 5 tasks completed |
| Tasks | Momentum | 20 tasks completed |
| Tasks | Unstoppable | 50 tasks completed |
| Addiction-free | 3-Day Clean | 3 days without blocked apps |
| Addiction-free | Week Clean | 7 days without blocked apps |
| Addiction-free | Month Clean | 30 days without blocked apps |

### Achievement System

Twelve achievements with multi-condition unlock criteria:

| Achievement | Requirement | XP Reward |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|
| First Steps | Complete 1 focus session | 50 |
| Perfect Week | 7 consecutive days of activity | 100 |
| Focus Streak 3 | 3-day focus streak | 50 |
| Focus Streak 7 | 7-day focus streak | 100 |
| Focus Streak 30 | 30-day focus streak | 200 |
| Total Sessions 10 | 10 focus sessions | 50 |
| Total Sessions 50 | 50 focus sessions | 100 |
| Total Sessions 100 | 100 focus sessions | 200 |
| Total Time 10h | 600 minutes focused | 100 |
| Total Time 50h | 3,000 minutes focused | 200 |
| Level 5 | Reach level 5 | 100 |
| Level 10 | Reach level 10 | 200 |

### Challenge System

Ten challenges in three tiers:

| Tier | Challenge | XP Reward | Duration |
|------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| Daily | Breathing Reset | 20 | 1 day |
| Daily | Focus Sprint | 50 | 1 day |
| Daily | No Doom Scrolling | 100 | 1 day |
| Daily | Journal Entry | 20 | 1 day |
| Weekly | 7-Day Warrior | 100 | 7 days |
| Weekly | 10 Focus Hours | 200 | 7 days |
| Weekly | Urge Master | 50 | 7 days |
| Special | Cold Shower Week | 100 | 7 days |
| Special | Dopamine Detox | 200 | 7 days |
| Special | Early to Bed | 50 | 7 days |

## Hardware and Infrastructure Specifications {#sec-hardware}

| Component | Specification | Purpose |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| Frontend | Flutter (Android/iOS) | Cross-platform mobile app |
| Backend Gateway | DigitalOcean Droplet, 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM | FastAPI microservices |
| Database | Supabase managed PostgreSQL | Auth, persistence, RLS |
| Object Storage | Supabase Storage | Audio files, images |
| Vector Database | ChromaDB (persistent) | Clinical knowledge retrieval |
| Embedding | `all-mpnet-base-v2` (768-dim) | Document/query encoding |
| GPU Inference (planned) | NVIDIA A100 80GB — RunPod Cloud | LLM, emotion classifier, Whisper |
| Local LLM (dev) | Ollama + `qwen2.5:7b` | Development/testing |
| Chat LLM (current) | Groq Cloud — `llama-3.1-8b-instant` | Production chat |
| Client Device | Android 10+ / iOS 15+, 4GB RAM | Minimum requirements |

## Security Considerations {#sec-security}

### Authentication and Authorization

-   **Three-tier JWT verification**: (1) HS256 with shared secret, (2) ES256 via JWKS endpoint, (3) unverified fallback for development.
-   **Supabase Auth**: Email/password with JWT tokens, OAuth2 support, and Row-Level Security (RLS) policies.
-   **Rate limiting**: `/api/assess` limited to 10 requests/minute via `slowapi`.

### Data Protection

-   **Encryption in transit**: TLS 1.3 for all API and database connections.
-   **Encryption at rest**: AES-256 via Supabase for database and storage.
-   **Data minimization**: Only necessary user data is collected; conversation logs are anonymized for analytics.
-   **Access control**: RLS policies enforce per-user data isolation; service role keys are restricted to backend services.

### Clinical Safety

-   **Crisis detection**: Bilingual keyword detection (English and Arabic) with hard-coded crisis vocabulary.
-   **Emergency protocol**: Crisis keywords trigger a mandatory redirect to emergency resources with locale-specific hotline numbers.
-   **Clinical auditor**: Every response is screened for safety before delivery; RED status blocks delivery and triggers emergency resources.
-   **Robotic tone detection**: Ensures responses are empathetic, not clinical or dismissive.
-   **Frustration scoring**: Detects user frustration and adjusts response tone or offers human support escalation.

### Compliance

-   **GDPR principles**: Data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, accuracy, integrity, confidentiality.
-   **Egyptian Data Protection Law**: Designed with local regulatory requirements in mind.
-   **Informed consent**: Users are informed about data collection practices and AI-generated nature of responses.
-   **Transparency**: Users can access and delete their conversation data at any time.
